prediction.reasoning: The price prediction and confidence intervals are derived using a Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model, a common stochastic model for asset prices. The key parameters used are: the current AAPL price ($264.58), a time horizon of 20 trading days (approximately 0.079 years, assuming 252 trading days per year), an assumed annual drift (average return) of 8%, and an estimated annual volatility of 28%. The point estimate represents the median of the log-normal distribution for the price after 20 days. The confidence intervals are calculated based on the volatility and quantiles of the standard normal distribution, reflecting the probabilistic range of future prices under this model.
